tr.td: Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
24 HR metoprolol succinate 50 MG Extended Release Oral Tablet [Toprol]
1 tablet (50 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (5 mg) by mouth once daily
Atorvastatin (as atorvastatin calcium) 10 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
Prasugrel (as prasugrel hydrochloride) 10 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (400 mg) every 8 hours as needed
1 tablet (25 mg) by mouth once daily
Clopidogrel (as clopidogrel bisulfate) 75 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (75 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (20 mg) by mouth once daily
400 micrograms (1 spray) sublingually as needed for chest pain, may repeat every 5 minutes as needed, up to 3 sprays in 15 minutes
Metoprolol tartrate (as metoprolol succinate) 100 mg prolonged-release oral tablet
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction (disorder)
Assessment of cardiac status using monitoring device
History of coronary artery bypass grafting (situation)
History of coronary artery bypass grafting (situation)
History of coronary artery bypass grafting (situation)
History of coronary artery bypass grafting (situation)
Transfusion of packed red blood cells
Abnormal findings diagnostic imaging heart+coronary circulat (finding)
Referral to home health care service
Inadequate digital access affecting health care

ul.li: Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mm[Hg] to reduce cardiovascular risk.
Patient will demonstrate understanding and implement recommended lifestyle modifications for hypertension, including dietary changes, physical activity, and sodium reduction.
Patient will implement and maintain a diabetes self-management plan to prevent progression of prediabetes.
Patient will achieve and maintain a healthy weight to reduce risk of diabetes progression.
Patient will maintain blood pressure within target range to support metabolic health.

tr.td: Vaccine product containing only Influenza virus antigen (medicinal product)
Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Blood by Immunoassay
Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported
Leukocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
Erythrocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood by Automated count
Erythrocyte distribution width [Ratio] by Automated count
Platelets [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
Urea nitrogen [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Aspartate aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
INR in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay
aPTT in Blood by Coagulation assay
Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Blood by Immunoassay
Troponin I.cardiac [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by High sensitivity method
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction

Patient will demonstrate understanding and implement recommended lifestyle modifications for hypertension, including dietary changes, physical activity, and sodium reduction.
